intro: This page presents the reviewed SC SuperLoop lead-and-control queue exported into SCLib.
The feed intentionally mixes known references, benchmark controls, parent comparators, conditional candidates, and DFT-screened leads under explicit front-visible labels.
Lane identity, required-condition semantics, and public-facing display classes are preserved so parent systems and known superconductors do not masquerade as equivalent new predictions.

filter_rules.label: Checker
filter_rules.value: verified / pass rows are reviewed; pending and revise remain visible but explicitly labeled

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Important bismuthate family anchor that should stay encoded as a doped oxide reference rather than be reduced to a generic conventional-metal analogy.
candidates.review_summary: Copper-free oxide superconducting reference with experimentally reported transition near 30 K. This record is explicitly the single-phase Ba0.6K0.4BiO3 perovskite reported in the discovery paper; do not flatten it into generic BaBiO3, arbitrary K-doped bismuthates, or oxygen-unconstrained mixtures.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Cava et al., Nature 332, 814-816 (1988), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Canonical 122 iron-based family anchor with doping-controlled superconductivity; wording stays in the correlated iron-pnictide lane rather than a simple conventional EPC label.
candidates.review_summary: Foundational hole-doped 122 iron-pnictide superconducting record. The discovery report is for potassium-substituted compositions around x = 0.4; parent BaFe2As2 and end-member KFe2As2 are not the same superconducting context encoded here.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Rotter et al., Physical Review Letters 101, 107006 (2008), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Useful framework superconductor reference for non-layered covalent-network branches; no stronger mechanism claim is made here.
candidates.review_summary: Clathrate superconductor baseline record for the light-framework branch. This baseline record refers to the [internal path] synthesized Ba8Si46 clathrate phase reported in the original discovery paper.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Yamanaka et al., Inorganic Chemistry 39, 56-58 (2000), doi:[internal path]

candidates.review_summary: Experimentally established Bi-2212 cuprate reference with well-known high-Tc layered superconductivity. This row refers to the oxygen-doped Bi2Sr2CaCu2O8+δ superconducting context; it should remain a literature anchor rather than a DFT-only exploratory row.
candidates.provenance_summary: secondary_experimental_literature: Hewat et al., Nature 333, 53-54 (1988), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Reference layered intercalation superconductor retained for family coverage and conventional-EPC-adjacent benchmarking.
candidates.review_summary: Bulk graphite-intercalation superconductor with experimentally reported transition at ambient pressure. This record refers to bulk rhombohedral CaC6 synthesized as a calcium-intercalated graphite compound; do not flatten it into generic graphite or generic calcium-carbon mixtures.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Emery et al., Physical Review Letters 95, 087003 (2005), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Useful higher-Tc fulleride anchor for the molecular-superconductor branch, with composition-sensitive density-of-states interpretation already noted in the discovery paper.
candidates.review_summary: Mixed-alkali fulleride reference with experimentally reported molecular-superconductor transition above the K3C60 baseline. The cited record is specifically for mixed Cs_xRb_yC60 prepared with dopant feed x = 2 and y = 1; this is not a claim about arbitrary Cs-only or Rb-only fulleride compositions.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Tanigaki et al., Nature 352, 222-223 (1991), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: High-Tc multilayer cuprate ceiling reference with strongly correlation-sensitive, doping-tuned superconductivity.
candidates.review_summary: Experimentally established Hg-1223 cuprate reference rather than a loop-generated exploratory row. The established superconducting phase is the oxygen-tuned Hg-1223 family usually written HgBa2Ca2Cu3O8+δ; this row should remain a literature anchor, not a new discovery candidate.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Vallcorba et al., Inorganic Chemistry 56, 11488-11495 (2017), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Canonical molecular-superconductor family anchor; keep the wording in the fulleride lane rather than forcing a simplified elemental or intermetallic analogy.
candidates.review_summary: Foundational alkali-fulleride superconducting record with experimentally reported transition in doped C60. The superconducting context is potassium-doped C60 in the K3C60 fulleride regime; the source distinguishes thin-film onset behavior from bulk Meissner evidence and should not be collapsed into undoped C60.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Hebard et al., Nature 350, 600-601 (1991), doi:[internal path]

candidates.review_summary: Experimentally established Sr-doped La2-xSrxCuO4 cuprate reference near the classic superconducting dome. This row refers to the explicitly Sr-doped superconducting LSCO context; it should remain a literature anchor rather than a DFT-only exploratory row.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Tarascon et al., Science 235, 1373-1376 (1987), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Discovery-era iron-pnictide anchor; mechanism remains in the correlated iron-based family and is not relabeled as simple conventional EPC.
candidates.review_summary: Foundational fluorine-doped iron-pnictide superconducting record. Superconductivity is reported for fluorine-doped compositions around x = 0.05-0.12; undoped LaOFeAs is not the superconducting state referenced here.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Kamihara et al., Journal of the American Chemical Society 130, 3296-3297 (2008), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: High-Tc member of the doped MNCl layered nitride-halide family; strongly condition-sensitive.
candidates.review_summary: Intercalation-induced superconductivity in the beta-HfNCl family. Superconductivity requires electron doping via lithium intercalation; parent beta-HfNCl is not the superconducting state referenced here.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Yamanaka et al., Nature 392, 580-582 (1998), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Layered nitride-halide family with doping-sensitive superconductivity; mechanism remains outside a simple MgB2-style proxy.
candidates.review_summary: Electron-doped layered nitride halide superconductor. Superconductivity requires lithium [internal path] doping; undoped beta-ZrNCl is not the superconducting state referenced here.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Yamanaka et al., Advanced Materials 8, 771-774 (1996), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Conservative low-temperature intermetallic reference retained for family coverage; literature discusses electron-phonon coupling with possible additional magnetic complexity.
candidates.review_summary: Low-Tc intermetallic superconducting reference with experimentally reported bulk transition. Ambient-pressure bulk Mo3Sb7 specific-heat literature context; this record does not generalize to pressure-tuned or Te-substituted variants.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Tran et al., Acta Materialia 56, 5694-5700 (2008), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Early higher-Tc 1111 iron-based reference showing the rapid rare-earth optimization of the discovery-era pnictide family.
candidates.review_summary: Rare-earth 1111 iron-pnictide record above the LaO1-xFxFeAs baseline. Superconductivity is reported for fluorine-doped SmFeAsO1-xFx; the undoped parent is not the superconducting state referenced here.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Chen et al., Nature 453, 761-762 (2008), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Multilayer thallium cuprate reference for the upper-Tc cuprate branch.
candidates.review_summary: Experimentally established Tl-2223 bulk cuprate reference, not a new bounded DFT-only candidate. This row refers to the established Tl-2223 superconducting phase reported in the experimental literature; it should not remain in the public corpus as an unconfirmed exploratory candidate.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Torardi et al., Science 240, 631-634 (1988), doi:[internal path]

candidates.review_summary: Experimentally established YBCO high-Tc cuprate reference above liquid-nitrogen temperature. This row refers to the oxygenated YBa2Cu3O7-δ superconducting context; it should remain a literature anchor rather than a DFT-only exploratory row.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Wu et al., Physical Review Letters 58, 908-910 (1987), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Historic heavy-fermion mechanism anchor showing superconductivity emerging in a strongly correlated Kondo-lattice environment.
candidates.review_summary: Foundational heavy-fermion superconducting record discovered in the late 1970s. This record refers to superconductivity in CeCu2Si2 itself and should remain in the heavy-fermion / strong-correlation lane rather than being collapsed into a simple low-Tc intermetallic baseline.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Steglich et al., Physical Review Letters 43, 1892-1896 (1979), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Kagome-system superconductivity intertwined with [internal path] phenomenology; mechanism should not be collapsed into a conventional EPC label.
candidates.review_summary: Ambient-pressure kagome superconductor with intertwined charge order and superconductivity. Ambient-pressure bulk [internal path] literature context; no doping or high-pressure condition encoded in this baseline record.
candidates.provenance_summary: secondary_experimental_literature: Gupta et al., Nano Letters 23 (2023), doi:[internal path]

candidates.review_summary: Prescreen-stage SC SuperLoop exploratory candidate retained as a bounded public research row, not as a confirmed superconductivity claim. This is a DFT-screened exploratory record only; public visibility does not imply a validated Tc claim.
candidates.provenance_summary: internal_dft_screen: SC SuperLoop prescreen candidate E0-2026-06-28-0215

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Topological-doped superconducting family anchor with unconventional pairing interest; do not collapse to a conventional EPC label.
candidates.review_summary: Topological-superconductor candidate family derived from Cu-intercalated Bi2Se3. Superconductivity is reported for copper-intercalated compositions around 0.12<=x<=0.15; undoped Bi2Se3 is not the superconducting state referenced here.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Hor et al., Physical Review Letters 104, 057001 (2010), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Minimal iron-chalcogenide superconducting family anchor; mechanism is not reduced here to a simple conventional EPC label.
candidates.review_summary: Ambient-pressure iron-based superconductor baseline in the FeSe family. The baseline superconducting record is the PbO-type alpha-FeSe report with intentional selenium deficiency; do not generalize this number to arbitrary pressure, film, or intercalated FeSe variants.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Hsu et al., Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences 105 (2008), doi:[internal path]

candidates.review_summary: Prescreen-stage SC SuperLoop exploratory candidate retained as a bounded public research row, not as a confirmed superconductivity claim. This is a DFT-screened exploratory record only; public visibility does not imply a validated Tc claim.
candidates.provenance_summary: internal_dft_screen: SC SuperLoop prescreen candidate E0-2026-06-28-0267

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Historic first high-Tc cuprate discovery; mechanism remains in the unconventional cuprate space and is not reduced here to a conventional EPC label.
candidates.review_summary: Foundational hole-doped cuprate superconductor record. The reported superconducting state is Ba-doped La-Cu-O; the parent undoped La2CuO4 is not the superconducting state referenced here.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Bednorz and Muller, Zeitschrift fur Physik B 64, 189-193 (1986), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Intrinsic 111 iron-based superconducting family anchor; mechanism remains in the iron-based unconventional space rather than a simple MgB2-style EPC picture.
candidates.review_summary: Ambient-pressure stoichiometric iron-pnictide superconductor. Reported for the stoichiometric LiFeAs system; this record does not encode pressure or substitution tuning.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Wang et al., Solid State Communications 148, 538-540 (2008), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Reference unconventional oxide superconductor outside the cuprate and iron-pnictide branches; pairing symmetry remains scientifically sensitive, so wording stays conservative.
candidates.review_summary: Ambient-pressure unconventional layered-oxide superconductor baseline. The discovery record refers to high-quality Sr2RuO4 crystals at ambient pressure; disorder strongly suppresses Tc and should remain explicit in later variants.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Maeno et al., Nature 372, 532-534 (1994), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Mechanism-complex heavy-fermion anchor retained to keep the unconventional strong-correlation branch explicit in the credible corpus.
candidates.review_summary: Early unconventional heavy-fermion superconducting record with sub-kelvin bulk transition. This record is for bulk UBe13 in its heavy-fermion superconducting context; do not flatten it into generic uranium-beryllium alloys or treat it as a conventional EPC control.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Ott et al., Physical Review Letters 50, 1595-1598 (1983), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Canonical high-Tc cuprate family anchor with oxygen-ordering sensitivity and unconventional pairing physics.
candidates.review_summary: Ambient-pressure high-Tc cuprate benchmark above liquid-nitrogen temperature. The literature discovery context is the oxygenated Y-Ba-Cu-O superconducting phase; oxygen deficiency strongly controls superconductivity and should not be flattened away.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Wu et al., Physical Review Letters 58, 908-910 (1987), doi:[internal path]

candidates.review_summary: Prescreen-stage SC SuperLoop exploratory candidate retained as a bounded public research row, not as a confirmed superconductivity claim. This is a DFT-screened exploratory record only; public visibility does not imply a validated Tc claim.
candidates.provenance_summary: internal_dft_screen: SC SuperLoop prescreen candidate E0-2026-06-28-0006

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Canonical two-gap [internal path] conventional EPC superconductor with strong E2g boron phonon coupling.
candidates.review_summary: Bulk conventional superconductor benchmark at ambient pressure. Ambient-pressure bulk MgB2 benchmark reference.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Nagamatsu et al., Nature 410, 63-64 (2001), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Canonical elemental type-II conventional superconductor used as a baseline control in many superconducting-device contexts.
candidates.review_summary: Bulk elemental superconducting reference for the highest-Tc elemental-metal baseline. Ambient-pressure bulk elemental Nb reference; cavity-grade surface treatments and alloy variants are not encoded here.
candidates.provenance_summary: secondary_experimental_literature: Roberts, Journal of Physical and Chemical Reference Data 5, 581-822 (1976), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: High-field conventional intermetallic superconductor and core A15 family anchor; retained here as a reference record rather than a novelty claim.
candidates.review_summary: Classic A15 intermetallic superconducting reference at ambient pressure. Baseline A15 Nb3Sn reference near stoichiometric composition; [internal path] tuning can shift the exact transition temperature.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Hardy and Hulm, Physical Review 95, 1435 (1954), doi:[internal path]

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Strong-coupling conventional elemental superconductor with important spin-orbit physics, but this record does not upgrade beyond conservative reference language.
candidates.review_summary: Bulk elemental superconducting reference at ambient pressure. Ambient-pressure bulk elemental Pb reference; thin-film and alloy variants are not encoded in this baseline record.
candidates.provenance_summary: secondary_experimental_literature: Roberts, Journal of Physical and Chemical Reference Data 5, 581-822 (1976), doi:[internal path]

candidates.review_summary: Prescreen-stage SC SuperLoop exploratory candidate retained as a bounded public research row, not as a confirmed superconductivity claim. This is a DFT-screened exploratory record only; public visibility does not imply a validated Tc claim.
candidates.provenance_summary: internal_dft_screen: SC SuperLoop prescreen candidate E0-2026-06-28-0031

candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: Finite DOS at E_F in the loop-verified fixed-cell path is consistent with a conventional metallic nitride interpretation.
candidates.review_summary: Loop-verified metallic nitride anchor; benchmark-adjacent conventional superconducting family. This reference context is film- and orientation-sensitive TiN; do not generalize the quoted Tc to arbitrary TiN morphologies or disorder regimes.
candidates.provenance_summary: primary_literature: Zhang et al., Applied Physics Letters 124, 182601 (2024), doi:[internal path]
